page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.large is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the ecs.s3 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, indicating that hyperthreading is disabled. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing an allocation of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage must be attached externally. It contains zero GPUs and does not provide any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Additionally, advanced CPU instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions and Advanced Matrix Extensions, as well as hardware-assisted cryptography extensions, are not enabled or supported on this hardware configuration.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is structured for compute-focused workloads that do not require large memory footprints or local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities, though the lack of advanced vector and matrix instruction sets limits its utility for modern vector-heavy mathematical computations or machine learning inference. It is suitable for basic web serving, lightweight application hosting, and utility computing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.large is an x86_64 server instance within the ecs.s3 family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, featuring a single thread per core and no hyperthreading. The instance provides 8.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core ratio. There is no local storage included, and the instance lacks GPU acceleration and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Advanced CPU instruction sets, including AVX, AVX512, AMX, and hardware cryptography extensions, are unavailable. This resource profile is designed for compute-oriented workloads with low memory requirements, such as basic web serving, lightweight application hosting, and general utility tasks that require dedicated CPU allocation without the need for high-performance vector processing or local disk space.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute instance featuring four physical cores, eight gigabytes of memory, and no local storage.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs on 4 physical cores with no hyperthreading
8.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for lightweight compute workloads and basic web serving
